message:

+    "description": "Provide a way to specify desired platform when creating packages and builders. Requested by 10+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current buildpacks setup",
+        "description": "Verify your buildpacks version and configuration:\n```bash\npack version\n```\nThis feature requires a working buildpacks installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review buildpacks configuration",
+        "description": "Review the relevant buildpacks configuration:\nWith the growing need for ARM64 images, it is a challenge to build them with the current tool support. It would be helpful if we had the ability to cross package buildpacks."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Provide a way to specify desired platform when creating…",
+        "description": "Hi all,\n\nMulti-architecture support for buildpacks have been requested (#1459 | #1460 ) for a long time. Recently I have been working on this as part of the Linux Foundation Mentorship program under the mentorship of Juan Bustamante (), Jerico Pena () and Aidan Delaney ()\n\nWe would appreciate your ideas and thoughts on how we want to move forward. There is a lot of validations and test to be added along the line. We are having a weekly meetings for multi-arch support every Monday @ 10:00 AM\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected.\nConfirm the feature described in \"Provide a way to specify desired platform when creating…\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Hi all,\n\nMulti-architecture support for buildpacks have been requested (#1459 | #1460 ) for a long time.
